what_it_does: A local ThreadingHTTPServer that renders cc_board documents as a mobile-first PWA review surface, lets an operator add paragraph/section/document-level comments (with optional images) back onto the board, and provides a real-time chat page with SSE push and operator self-registration as a channel member.
observations: Serves document review pages and a separate /chat page from inline HTML/CSS/JS templates.
Uses Server-Sent Events (/chat-stream) to push new messages, status, and a per-process version to connected browsers.
Stores comments with a structured envelope like [paragraph] re: «selected text»\n\nbody and hides that syntax when rendering.
Registers the running app as channel member 'tim' by writing a JSON file in .data/channels/.
Binds to 127.0.0.1 and expects Tailscale serve for phone reachability.
Includes a custom markdown-to-HTML parser rather than an external library.
Supports image attachments in both comments and chat by saving base64 images via cc_images.
Deletes comments by removing a .md file directly from cb.NOTICEBOARD_DIR.
standouts: Realtime two-way surface: browser SSE plus channel push receiver on POST /.
Structured comment envelope with scale chip and hidden anchor quote.
Self-registering operator presence ('tim') so the fabric can push replies into the app.
PWA-ready with manifest, theme color, safe-area insets, and DNA-style SVG icons.
conventions: Hardcoded channel and default document constants at module level.
Global mutable SSE_CLIENTS list protected by threading.Lock.
Inline client code in large triple-quoted Python strings with placeholder substitution.
Broad try/except blocks with noqa markers for lint suppression.
concerns: comment-delete bypasses the store abstraction by deleting a .md file directly from cb.NOTICEBOARD_DIR.
Hardcoded CHANNEL and DEFAULT_DOC limit the 'reusable' claim in the docstring.
VERSION changes every process restart, forcing client reloads even when code is unchanged.
Chat delivery to the lead depends on an external _chat_lead.txt file with no visible fallback.
parse_comment regex can fail on edge cases; edit path may then drop the original scale/quote anchor.
Large embedded JavaScript/CSS is hard to unit test and maintain.
notes: A self-contained, phone-first review-and-chat server that turns board documents into a commentable PWA and registers itself as a pushable channel endpoint.
questions: What contracts do cc_board, cc_images, and cc_channels expose (argument/return shapes)?
How is the lead target file _chat_lead.txt created and updated?
What happens when --author differs from the hardcoded OPERATOR_HANDLE 'tim'?
Is direct file deletion in comment-delete intentional or a temporary workaround?
Are there tests for the custom markdown parser and SSE behavior?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly matches: it is a local mobile review surface with PWA support and two-way chat. The 'reusable' claim is weakened by hardcoded channel and default document, and the storage abstraction is bypassed for deletes.
intention_signals: The file is trying to give a human operator a phone-native window into board documents and chat, where every tap granularity becomes a structured comment anchored to the source text, and the app itself is a first-class channel member that can receive pushes from the fabric.
novelty: The combination of per-paragraph/section/whole-document commenting via hidden scale/quote envelopes, self-registration as a pushable channel member, and SSE-based live reload in a single-file local server.
contribution: A self-contained mobile review and chat server that renders board documents, captures structured comments with image attachments, provides a real-time chat surface, and registers itself as a pushable channel endpoint.
summary_for_embedding: ops/doc_review_server.py is a single-file local HTTP server that renders cc_board documents as a mobile-first PWA review surface, supports paragraph/section/document-level structured commenting with image attachments, and provides a real-time chat interface backed by SSE and channel self-registration.
intention_for_embedding: To give a phone-reachable operator a lightweight, two-way review and chat surface over the company's board/document fabric without polling.

symbols.does: Pushes a JSON payload to every connected SSE client queue.
symbols.description: Iterates the global SSE_CLIENTS list under a lock and enqueues the payload for each client's SSE handler to flush. This is the server's outbound broadcast path for live messages, status, and version events.

symbols.does: Formats a stored message record into an SSE payload.
symbols.description: Maps the record author to 'You' or 'Vi', copies the body, and collects attachment image URLs from links of kind 'attachment'. The result is what /chat-stream delivers to browsers.

symbols.does: Writes a JSON presence file for handle 'tim' in .data/channels/.
symbols.description: Records the app's port, pid, cwd, and transport so the channel fabric can route replies to 'tim' via HTTP POST to this server. It makes the app a pushable channel member.

symbols.does: Escapes HTML and applies inline markdown formatting.
symbols.description: Replaces &, <, > with entities, then converts backtick code, **bold**, and *italic* markdown into corresponding HTML tags. Used by the custom markdown renderer.

symbols.does: Converts a subset of Markdown into HTML.
symbols.description: Parses headings, horizontal rules, blockquotes, unordered lists, ordered lists, and paragraphs line-by-line, calling _inline for inline formatting. It is a lightweight replacement for a full Markdown library.

symbols.does: Splits a stored comment body into scale, quote, and human text.
symbols.description: Uses a regex to extract the [scale] chip, optional re: «quote» anchor, and the remaining text so the raw envelope syntax is never shown in the UI. Falls back to returning the whole body as text if the pattern does not match.

symbols.does: Recursively renders a comment thread as HTML.
symbols.description: Builds nested <div class='comment'> elements with author styling, scale chips, edit/delete controls, and attached images. Calls itself for nested replies.

symbols.does: Returns a short display label from a document title.
symbols.description: Takes the portion before an em dash, or the full title, and truncates to 32 characters. Used for the top-bar title in the review page.

symbols.does: Scans the board once and builds lookup maps.
symbols.description: Returns a tuple of items keyed by address, a reverse edge map from (target, kind) to source items, and the list of documents in the configured channel. This avoids repeated reverse traversals per block.

symbols.does: Collects top-level comments and nested replies for an address.
symbols.description: Returns comments linked with kind 'commented_on' to the address, and recursively nests replies linked with kind 'reply_to'.

symbols.does: Recursively gathers reply threads for a comment address.
symbols.description: Inner helper of _thread that walks the reverse edge map's 'reply_to' links to build nested reply lists.

symbols.does: Builds the hamburger drawer navigation HTML.
symbols.description: Emits a chat link and a list of document links, marking the current document with a 'current' class. It uses placeholder substitution and a hack to remove an emoji from the chat link text.

symbols.does: Renders the full HTML page for a given document id.
symbols.description: Builds the index, resolves the document and its ordered blocks, renders each block with its comments, and substitutes placeholders in the PAGE template. It also includes the whole-document comment thread in the expanded header panel.

symbols.does: Counts comments recursively in a thread.
symbols.description: Inner helper that sums each comment plus the count of its nested replies, used to populate the comment-count dot on each block.

symbols.does: Renders the chat page HTML.
symbols.description: Loads messages of type 'message' in the configured channel, sorts by created time, and substitutes message bubbles into the CHAT_PAGE template.

symbols.does: HTTP request handler for review, chat, assets, SSE, and POST endpoints.
symbols.description: Subclass of BaseHTTPRequestHandler that serves the document review page, chat page, icons, images, web manifest, an SSE stream, and JSON endpoints for comments and chat messages.

symbols.does: Suppresses default request logging.
symbols.description: Overrides BaseHTTPRequestHandler.log_message to do nothing, keeping the console quiet during operation.

symbols.does: Writes an HTTP response with status, headers, and body.
symbols.description: Encodes string bodies as UTF-8, sets Content-Type and Content-Length, and swallows BrokenPipeError/ConnectionResetError when the client disconnects early.

symbols.does: Serves a static file from disk.
symbols.description: Opens the given path in binary mode and returns its contents with the supplied MIME type, or a 404 if the file cannot be read.

symbols.does: Routes GET requests to icons, images, manifest, SSE, chat, and document pages.
symbols.description: Handles PWA assets, image lookups via cc_images, the manifest, the /chat-stream SSE endpoint, /chat, and /doc/<id> (or / for the default document). Errors are returned as a simple HTML pre block.

symbols.does: Routes POST endpoints for channel injection, chat, comments, edits, and deletes.
symbols.description: Parses JSON bodies, stores messages and comments through cc_board, saves attached images via cc_images, forwards chat messages to the lead target via cc_channels, and deletes comments by removing their .md file. Returns JSON ok/error responses.

symbols.does: Parses arguments and starts the HTTP server.
symbols.description: Reads --port and --author, updates the global AUTHOR, registers the operator presence, and runs ThreadingHTTPServer on 127.0.0.1.
